title: Numbers and Time in English
description: Say the numbers you use every day!

scenes.speech: Numbers are super important in English too! Every day! ⏰
scenes.title: Into Numbers and Time
scenes.content: This lesson is about numbers and time in English. Prices, time, age, dates — saying numbers opens every part of daily life.

scenes.title: 1 to 10
scenes.content: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten. Useful in game chat or sharing snacks.
scenes.highlight:
1 one  2 two  3 three  4 four  5 five
6 six  7 seven  8 eight  9 nine  10 ten

scenes.title: 11 to 20
scenes.content: 11 eleven, 12 twelve, 13 thirteen — from there the suffix becomes -teen. Saying them aloud makes the pattern stick.

scenes.speech: Up to 100 and 1000 — that's the goal!
scenes.title: Big Numbers
scenes.content: 100 one hundred, 1000 one thousand. Hyphenate twenty-one, thirty-five — easier to read.

scenes.speech: Two ways to say the time!
scenes.title: Telling Time
scenes.content: It's seven o'clock. It's seven thirty. You can read the digits straight, or split into hour and minute.

scenes.speech: Days of the week, learned with numbers!
scenes.title: Days of the Week
scenes.content: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. The week starts on Monday in many calendars.

scenes.title: Months and Dates
scenes.content: From January to December. Say 'It's April 30.' — month then day. Great for birthdays.

scenes.speech: Reading prices in English looks cool!
scenes.title: Prices
scenes.content: $5 = five dollars. $9.99 = nine ninety-nine. Use $ and ¢ for dollars and cents.

scenes.title: Adding Units
scenes.content: I'm 10 years old. It's 3 kilometers. 5 minutes. Pair numbers with units and you sound natural.

scenes.speech: Quiz! How do you say 7:30 in English?
scenes.quizQuestion: Which is the most natural way to say '7:30'?
scenes.quizExplanation: It's seven thirty. is the simplest. 'It's half past seven.' also works.

scenes.speech: You're a number master! Use them daily! 🌟
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
